prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(register_cover_crop_field source_field_parcel_a)
(allocate_cover_crop_seed_lot source_field_parcel_a cover_crop_seed_lot_a)
(assign_cover_crop_species source_field_parcel_a cover_crop_species_a)
(authorize_cover_crop_field source_field_parcel_a cover_crop_species_a)
(assign_contractor_unit_to_field source_field_parcel_a contractor_unit_a)
(authorize_seeding_method source_field_parcel_a seeding_method_a cover_crop_species_a)
(commit_source_parcel source_field_parcel_a seeding_method_a contractor_unit_a)
(release_contractor_unit source_field_parcel_a contractor_unit_a)
(release_cover_crop_species source_field_parcel_a cover_crop_species_a)
(register_cover_crop_field target_field_parcel_a)
(allocate_cover_crop_seed_lot target_field_parcel_a cover_crop_seed_lot_b)
(assign_cover_crop_species target_field_parcel_a cover_crop_species_a)
(authorize_cover_crop_field target_field_parcel_a cover_crop_species_a)
(authorize_target_parcel target_field_parcel_a field_sector_a cover_crop_species_a)
(assign_contractor_unit_to_field target_field_parcel_a contractor_unit_a)
(commit_target_parcel target_field_parcel_a field_sector_a contractor_unit_a)
(release_cover_crop_species target_field_parcel_a cover_crop_species_a)
(release_contractor_unit target_field_parcel_a contractor_unit_a)
(assemble_work_order source_field_parcel_a target_field_parcel_a seeding_method_a field_sector_a cover_crop_work_order_a)
(assign_cover_crop_species source_field_parcel_a cover_crop_species_a)
(approve_work_order cover_crop_work_order_a source_field_parcel_a cover_crop_species_a)
(mark_source_parcel_execution_complete source_field_parcel_a cover_crop_work_order_a)
(mark_target_parcel_execution_complete target_field_parcel_a cover_crop_work_order_a)
(certify_field_closeout source_field_parcel_a regulatory_permit_a cover_crop_species_a)
(release_source_parcel_resources source_field_parcel_a cover_crop_seed_lot_a regulatory_permit_a)
(release_cover_crop_species source_field_parcel_a cover_crop_species_a)
(assign_cover_crop_species target_field_parcel_a cover_crop_species_a)
(certify_field_closeout target_field_parcel_a regulatory_permit_a cover_crop_species_a)
(release_target_parcel_resources target_field_parcel_a cover_crop_seed_lot_b regulatory_permit_a)
(register_cover_crop_field field_management_plan_a)
(allocate_cover_crop_seed_lot field_management_plan_a cover_crop_seed_lot_c)
(assign_cover_crop_species field_management_plan_a cover_crop_species_a)
(authorize_cover_crop_field field_management_plan_a cover_crop_species_a)
(stage_seed_package_for_work_order field_management_plan_a seed_package_a cover_crop_work_order_a)
(certify_seed_package_loaded field_management_plan_a seed_package_a cover_crop_work_order_a cover_crop_species_a)
(assign_contractor_unit_to_field field_management_plan_a contractor_unit_a)
(assign_inspection_checkpoint_to_plan field_management_plan_a inspection_checkpoint_a)
(assign_residue_management_method_to_plan field_management_plan_a residue_management_method_a)
(advance_plan_execution_route_a field_management_plan_a inspection_checkpoint_a contractor_unit_a seed_package_a cover_crop_work_order_a)
(stage_plan_execution_route_a field_management_plan_a residue_management_method_a seed_package_a cover_crop_work_order_a)
(close_plan_execution_direct field_management_plan_a)
(release_contractor_unit field_management_plan_a contractor_unit_a)
(remove_inspection_checkpoint_from_plan field_management_plan_a inspection_checkpoint_a)
(remove_residue_management_method_from_plan field_management_plan_a residue_management_method_a)
(certify_field_closeout field_management_plan_a regulatory_permit_a cover_crop_species_a)
(close_management_plan_and_release_resources field_management_plan_a cover_crop_seed_lot_c regulatory_permit_a)
(release_cover_crop_species field_management_plan_a cover_crop_species_a)
(register_cover_crop_field field_management_plan_b)
(allocate_cover_crop_seed_lot field_management_plan_b cover_crop_seed_lot_a)
(assign_cover_crop_species field_management_plan_b cover_crop_species_a)
(authorize_cover_crop_field field_management_plan_b cover_crop_species_a)
(stage_seed_package_for_work_order field_management_plan_b seed_package_b cover_crop_work_order_a)
(certify_seed_package_loaded field_management_plan_b seed_package_b cover_crop_work_order_a cover_crop_species_a)
(assign_contractor_unit_to_field field_management_plan_b contractor_unit_a)
(assign_inspection_checkpoint_to_plan field_management_plan_b inspection_checkpoint_a)
(assign_residue_management_method_to_plan field_management_plan_b residue_management_method_a)
(advance_plan_execution_route_a field_management_plan_b inspection_checkpoint_a contractor_unit_a seed_package_b cover_crop_work_order_a)
(stage_plan_execution_route_a field_management_plan_b residue_management_method_a seed_package_b cover_crop_work_order_a)
(close_plan_execution_direct field_management_plan_b)
(release_contractor_unit field_management_plan_b contractor_unit_a)
(remove_inspection_checkpoint_from_plan field_management_plan_b inspection_checkpoint_a)
(remove_residue_management_method_from_plan field_management_plan_b residue_management_method_a)
(certify_field_closeout field_management_plan_b regulatory_permit_a cover_crop_species_a)
(close_management_plan_and_release_resources field_management_plan_b cover_crop_seed_lot_a regulatory_permit_a)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem involves planning for cover crop insertion between main cycles using PDDL. I need to output only the sequence of ground actions that solve the problem.


## Understanding Initial Setup  
- The problem focuses on planning cover crop insertion between main crop cycles using PDDL.  
- The goal is to output only the sequence of ground actions needed to solve the problem.

## Examining Initial Objects  
- Objects include source_field_parcel_a, target_field_parcel_a, two field_management_plans (a and b), seed lots, species, contractor, and more.  
- Initial facts cover availabilities like fertilizer, operators, permits, and inspections, with specific links between parcels, plans, and work orders.

## Analyzing Plan Links  
- Both field_management_plans (a and b) are linked to source_field_parcel_a, target_field_parcel_a, and cover_crop_work_order_a.  
- Plan_a uses seed_package_a and termination_method_a, while plan_b uses seed_package_b, but termination_method_b is not available initially.

## Checking Goal Requirements  
- The goal is to close out field units for source_field_parcel_a, target_field_parcel_a, and both management plans.  
- Closing out requires steps like enrollment, authorization, execution_complete, closeout_verified, and resource release, starting from scratch.

## Identifying Field Unit Requirements  
- Field units, including parcels and plans, need to be enrolled, have a seed lot allocated, a species assigned, and authorized to proceed.  
- Authorization requires the field unit to be enrolled, have a committed seed lot, and an assigned cover crop species.  

## Examining Crop Assignment Challenges  
- All four field units (two parcels and two plans) need a crop assigned to certify closeout, but only one species is initially available.  
- Assigning a species makes it unavailable, suggesting sequential assignment with possible release via release_cover_crop_species to reuse for others.  

## Exploring Execution and Closeout Paths  
- Closing out requires execution_complete and field_crop_assigned for certification, with crop assignment needed at the time of certifying.  
- Plans and parcels have complex paths involving committing, staging, and confirming readiness, with actions like stage_seed_package and confirm_source_parcel_ready.  

## Analyzing Termination for Plans  
- Plans need termination methods linked for closeout, with plan_a already linked to available termination_method_a, but plan_b linked to unavailable termination_method_b.  
- Linking a termination method to a plan requires the plan to be authorized, have a crop assigned, and a termination method available.

## Examining Seed Lot Constraints  
- Only three cover crop seed lots (a, b, c) are available for four field units, including two parcels and two plans.  
- All field units require a committed seed lot to become authorized, a prerequisite for execution_complete and closeout.  

## Exploring Authorization Requirements  
- Actions like authorize_cover_crop_field, commit_source_parcel, and stage_seed_package all require the field unit to be authorized.  
- Authorization necessitates a committed seed lot and assigned crop, making it impossible to bypass for any field unit.  

## Analyzing Resource Sharing  
- Found that seed lots and permits can be released after closeout_verified, making them available for reuse.  
- This suggests processing field units sequentially, closing out one to release resources before allocating to the next.  

## Checking Sequential Processing  
- Regulatory permit (a) is also limited to one, requiring the same sequential release and reuse strategy as seed lots.  
- Sequential processing involves completing and closing out one field unit, then using released resources for the next, given shared dependencies between plans and parcels.

## Examining Parcel Processing  
- Authorizing source parcels requires contractor assignment, like contractor_a, and linking readiness, with commitment marking source_parcel_ready.  
- For target parcels, authorize_target_parcel opens the sector and requires crop assignment, with commit_target_parcel needing contractor assignment, which may require releasing from source first.  

## Checking Contractor Assignment Flow  
- Contractor must be assigned to source for commit, then released to become available for target assignment, ensuring sequential handling.  
- After committing source, contractor can be released, suggesting flexibility in timing for reassignment to targets.  

## Analyzing Work Order Assembly  
- Assembling work orders needs both source and target committed, with linked methods ready and sectors open, moving from draft to created status.  
- Approving work orders requires source commitment and crop assignment, using source parcel details for verification.  

## Exploring Plan Execution Paths  
- Marking execution complete for parcels involves mark_source and mark_target, requiring committed and ready states, but plans need separate closeout actions.  
- Closing out parcels after execution_complete involves certifying with available permits, then releasing resources like seed lots and permits for reuse.

## Continuing Plan Execution Paths  
- Staging a seed package for a work order requires the work order to be created and approved, with no source or target clearance for route_a.  
- Certifying a seed package loaded can prepare a plan for execution, matching route_a's need for no source clearance, using authorized plans and staged packages.  

## Examining Species Assignment Flow  
- Authorizing cover crop fields requires species assignment, seed commitment, and enrollment, making species release possible afterward.  
- Releasing species after authorization makes them available again, but approving work orders later requires reassigning the species to the source.

## Continuing Plan Execution Paths
- Authorizing a target parcel requires first authorizing the cover crop field, ensuring the target is enrolled, seed committed, and species assigned.
- After general authorization, authorize_target_parcel opens the sector, linking it and marking it ready for contractor assignment.

## Managing Contractor and Commitment
- Assigning a contractor to the target parcel requires the sector to be open and authorized, enabling commit_target_parcel to mark it ready.
- Once both source and target are committed and ready, assembling the work order moves it from draft to created status, linking methods and sectors.

## Handling Species Reassignment
- Approving the work order requires the crop species to be assigned to the source, but if currently assigned to the target, it must be released first.
- Releasing the species from the target makes it available, allowing reassignment to the source for work order approval.

## Advancing Plan Execution

- Staging a seed package for plan_a binds it to the work order, requiring created and approved status without needing clearances, aligning with route_a's needs.
- Certifying the seed package loaded prepares plan_a for execution, ensuring it's linked, staged, and bound, with no work order source clearance required.

## Linking Inspections and Residue

- Assigning an inspection checkpoint to plan_a links it for advancement, requiring an available inspection and authorized plan.
- Linking a residue management method to plan_a prepares for execution, ensuring the method is available and the plan is authorized.

## Finalizing Plan Execution

- Advancing plan execution sets plan_a to in_execution, requiring prepared status, linked inspection, contractor, and package, with no target clearance.
- Closing plan execution finalizes plan_a as execution_complete, needing execution_ready status and no staging, completing the process.

## Releasing and Assigning Species
- Releasing the cover crop species from plan_a makes it available for reassignment to plan_b.
- Assigning the species to plan_b enables authorization and further execution steps for plan_b.

## Staging and Preparing Plan_b
- Staging a seed package for plan_b binds it to the work order, ensuring availability and alignment for execution.
- Certifying the seed package loaded marks plan_b as prepared, meeting requirements like no work order source clearance.

## Advancing Plan_b Execution
- Assigning contractor, inspection, and residue methods to plan_b prepares it for execution, ensuring all components are linked and available.
- Advancing plan execution sets plan_b to in_execution, requiring prepared status and linked resources without target clearance.

## Finalizing Plan_b
- Closing plan execution finalizes plan_b as execution_complete, needing execution_ready status and no staging.
- Certifying field closeout verifies plan_b, linking it to a permit and making resources available for release.

## Managing Resources and Closure
- Closing the management plan releases resources like seed lots and permits, marking plan_b as closed_out.
- All plans (source, target, plan_a, plan_b) are now closed, with resources recycled or shared as needed across the process.

## Managing Plan_a Execution

- Certifying the seed package for plan_a loads it, linking to the work order and species, preparing for execution.
- Assigning contractor, inspection, and residue methods to plan_a ensures all resources are ready for advancement.

## Staging Plan_a

- Staging plan execution for plan_a involves linking residue methods and seed packages to the work order, advancing readiness.
- Closing plan execution directly finalizes plan_a, marking it as execution_complete and ready for resource release.

## Releasing Plan_a Resources

- Releasing the contractor unit and removing inspection and residue methods from plan_a frees up resources for reuse.
- Certifying field closeout for plan_a verifies completion, linking to a permit and maintaining species assignment.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
